data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260510T211118-ec3a7a14/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260510T211118-ec3a7a14/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260510T211118-ec3a7a14/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260510T211118-ec3a7a14/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260510T211118-ec3a7a14/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-8645c5d4">
    <title>feat(escalation): per-bead cost budget gate stops escalation when exceeded</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  Today's cost cap is ONLY a global drain-level CostCapTracker
  (cli/internal/escalation/infrastructure.go:128). There is no
  per-bead budget. A bead that fails repeatedly can burn unbounded
  budget escalating up the ladder before the global cap fires —
  one expensive bead can exhaust the entire drain's budget while
  cheap beads sit unworked.

  ADR-024 P1 (cheapest first, sister bead at rank 19) only works
  in practice if there is a per-bead "stop" condition to bound the
  escalation cost. Without it, "cheapest first" devolves into
  "cheapest first, then the most expensive thing imaginable,
  infinitely."

  Per ADR-024 Operator escape hatches subsection (sister bead
  rank 19), operators must be able to override the per-bead
  budget on a specific bead via a label budget:<USD>.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/internal/escalation/infrastructure.go:128 CostCapTracker is
    a single drain-level accumulator.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go and
    cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation.go runEscalatingSingleTierAttempts
    do NOT instantiate per-bead trackers.
  - There's no --max-bead-cost flag.
  - There's no budget:<USD> label override mechanism.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Add a new flag --max-bead-cost (default $5; configurable):
    cli/cmd/work.go (or wherever drain flags live).
  - cli/internal/escalation/infrastructure.go: rename
    CostCapTracker to GlobalCostCapTracker (or split). Add a
    PerBeadCostTracker that takes a budget at construction.
  - cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation.go
    runEscalatingSingleTierAttempts: instantiate a
    PerBeadCostTracker per bead claim, accumulate per-attempt cost,
    stop escalation (return ErrPerBeadBudgetExceeded) when budget
    is exceeded.
  - Operator escape hatch: when a bead has label budget:<USD>
    (e.g. budget:25.0), use that as the per-bead budget instead
    of --max-bead-cost. Parse from labels; emit a budget-override
    event when used.
  - On per-bead budget exhaustion: unclaim, leave bead open, log
    a "per_bead_budget_exhausted" event with cost details (matches
    TD-031 §5 row defined in sister bead rank 19). NOT a cooldown.

  Tests:
     - TestPerBeadCostTracker_StopsEscalationAtLimit
     - TestPerBeadCostTracker_NoLimit_RunsToLadderExhaustion
     - TestPerBeadCostTracker_BudgetLabelOverridesDefault: bead
       with label "budget:25.0" uses $25 instead of default $5.
     - TestPerBeadCostTracker_InvalidBudgetLabel_FallsBackToDefault.

NON-SCOPE
  - Adjusting per-bead default ($5 is starting point; tunable).
  - Smart per-bead budgeting (e.g., complexity-based) — out of
    scope.
  - The recovery:manual label (sister bead rank 25 handles).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead "docs(specs)" rank 19 — Per-bead budget +
    Operator escape hatches subsections.
  - Sister bead "auto-reframe + auto-decompose" rank 25 — recovery
    hooks count against the per-bead budget; if budget exceeded
    before reframe fires, fall through to per-bead-exhaustion.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/cmd/work.go (or equivalent) exposes a --max-bead-cost flag (default $5, configurable; 0 = unlimited).
2. cli/internal/escalation/infrastructure.go contains a PerBeadCostTracker (alongside the existing global CostCapTracker).
3. runEscalatingSingleTierAttempts instantiates a PerBeadCostTracker per bead and stops escalation when the budget is exceeded.
4. When a bead has a label of the form budget:<float>, that value overrides the default per-bead budget.
5. On budget exhaustion: (a) bead is unclaimed; (b) "per_bead_budget_exhausted" event is appended (matches TD-031 §5 row); (c) consecutive_ladder_exhaustions is incremented (chains into sister bead ddx-63155d5c auto-recovery trigger); (d) NO cooldown is set.
6. The auto-recovery hook (sister bead ddx-63155d5c) reads consecutive_ladder_exhaustions and treats per_bead_budget_exhausted as a valid trigger source (alongside ladder exhaustion).
7. TestPerBeadCostTracker_StopsEscalationAtLimit, TestPerBeadCostTracker_NoLimit_RunsToLadderExhaustion, TestPerBeadCostTracker_BudgetLabelOverridesDefault, TestPerBeadCostTracker_InvalidBudgetLabel_FallsBackToDefault, TestPerBeadCostTracker_ChainsIntoAutoRecovery all pass.
8. cd cli && go test ./internal/escalation/... ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:agent, kind:feature, spec:ADR-024, prevention</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="df6cd3c39b121afada591257c3adcd78025b4b90" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260510T211118-ec3a7a14"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All ACs satisfied and committed. Here's a summary of what was delivered:

**AC1** — `--max-bead-cost` flag in `work.go` (default `$5`, `0` = unlimited).

**AC2** — `PerBeadCostTracker` in `escalation/infrastructure.go` alongside `CostCapTracker`, with `ParseBeadBudgetLabel` and `PerBeadBudgetExhaustedReason`.

**AC3** — `runEscalatingSingleTierAttempts` takes a `*PerBeadCostTracker`, accumulates cost after each attempt, and halts before escalating to the next tier when the budget is exceeded.

**AC4** — The executor closure in `agent_cmd.go` checks `ParseBeadBudgetLabel(targetBead.Labels)` and uses that value if present instead of `spec.MaxBeadCostUSD`.

**AC5** — On budget exhaustion: (a) bead is unclaimed; (b) `per-bead-budget-exhausted` event appended with `total_cost` body; (c) `consecutive_ladder_exhaustions` incremented; (d) no cooldown set.

**AC6** — `consecutive_ladder_exhaustions` is the shared key used by the auto-recovery hook; `per_bead_budget_exhausted` increments it identically to ladder exhaustion.

**AC7/8/9** — All 5 required tests pass; full `./internal/escalation/... ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/...` suite green; `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
